title: BUGONIA
logline: A delusional warehouse worker kidnaps a pharmaceutical CEO he believes is an alien from Andromeda, subjecting her to increasingly unhinged interrogations—until she reveals she actually is extraterrestrial, and humanity's extinction is imminent.
antagonistOrConflict: Michelle Fuller / Teddy's delusion vs. reality (which inverts)
tone: Darkly comedic, increasingly disturbing, ultimately apocalyptic. Oscillates between absurdist humor and genuine psychological horror before landing in cosmic nihilism.
themes: The fragility of sanity and truth

actBreaks.description: Michelle wakes up captive in Teddy's basement and the interrogation begins—the central dramatic situation is now locked in place.

validation.ruling: Scene #44 (p16, 15.5%) ends Act 1 as Teddy and Don load unconscious Michelle into her car, don EMF-protection beanies, and drive away with their captive. This is an irreversible plot twist that fundamentally changes the story's direction from setup to captivity thriller, forces all characters into new high-stakes conflict, and represents a point of no return—the story cannot revert to its pre-kidnapping state.

actBreaks.description: Teddy discovers Michelle has escaped her restraints and explored his research room—the power dynamic has fundamentally shifted and Michelle is no longer purely victim.

validation.ruling: Scene #114 (p77, 75%) ends Act 2 with Michelle in shock, blood splattered on her face, and Don's dead body on the floor after his suicide. This is a major irreversible turning point that fundamentally transforms the story from psychological captivity drama to life-or-death crisis, eliminates Michelle's potential ally, and propels the narrative into its final act where Teddy's instability becomes lethal.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script employs a deceptive three-act structure that appears to follow kidnapping-thriller conventions before systematically dismantling them. Act One (scenes 1-44) is an extended, methodical setup contrasting Michelle's corporate perfection with Teddy's conspiracy-addled desperation, culminating in the abduction. Act Two (scenes 45-114) functions as a pressure-cooker interrogation that slowly reveals Teddy's tragic backstory while Michelle manipulates his cousin Don, building to Don's suicide and Michelle's partial escape. Act Three (scenes 115-184) executes a reality-inverting twist: Michelle actually IS an alien, Teddy was correct all along, and the script pivots from psychological thriller to apocalyptic sci-fi, ending with humanity's complete extinction. The structure is deliberately patient in its first two acts to make the final act's genre rupture more devastating.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is unusually deliberate for a thriller, with 184 scenes across 103 pages (averaging 0.56 pages per scene). The opening 40 scenes are rapid cross-cutting between Michelle and Teddy's morning routines, establishing parallel lives before collision. The basement interrogation sequences (scenes 44-114) slow considerably, allowing extended dialogue scenes that build psychological tension. The script accelerates dramatically after scene 114, with Teddy's mother's death (scenes 94-109) and the final confrontation (scenes 114-123) compressed into rapid beats. The apocalyptic montage (scenes 133A-133V) uses 23 brief scenes across 3 pages to depict global extinction, creating a breathless, horrifying finale.

incitingIncident.description: Michelle gets out of her car and is jumped by Teddy and Don in ski jackets and Halloween masks, but she throws Teddy off with surprising strength, gives Don a Krav Maga elbow strike to the throat that drops him choking, runs for the door but realizes she's lost her keys, then runs around the side of the house before Teddy tackles her and sprays Benadryl in her face until she passes out.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: EXT. MICHELLE’S MANSION - FRONT DRIVE. DAY 30

validation.ruling: Scene #30 is merely a transition shot between stores with no dramatic action. Scene #43 (p16, 16%) is the true inciting incident: Michelle is violently kidnapped by Teddy and Don in masks, fights back with surprising strength, but is ultimately subdued with Benadryl spray. This shatters the status quo, establishes the central conflict (captive/captor dynamic), introduces irreversible stakes, and forces both protagonist and antagonist into the main dramatic situation from which the entire story unfolds.

midpoint.description: Teddy plays back Michelle's sarcastic recording claiming to be an alien, rejects it as inadequate, and when she refuses to comply seriously, he violently attacks her with the shotgun butt, beating her repeatedly while Don watches in horror.

validation.ruling: Scene #85 (p40, 39%) functions as the midpoint where Teddy confronts Michelle with her recorded 'confession,' she refuses to cooperate convincingly, and he smashes the tape recorder in frustration. This marks Teddy's shift from believing his plan will work smoothly to recognizing Michelle won't comply, raising the stakes and forcing him toward more desperate measures. Michelle's corporate training and calm resistance establishes this as a point of no return in their psychological battle.

climax.description: Michelle and Teddy enter her office; he locks the door, barricades it with a couch, and closes the blinds; Michelle claims they will make remote contact with the emperor using a calculator with a 58-digit code, and begins typing while Teddy watches incredulously.

validation.ruling: Scene #122 shows Teddy merely entering his mother's room—setup, not confrontation. Scene #149 (p96, 93%) is the true climax: Michelle confronts Teddy in her office at Auxolith headquarters after escaping captivity. This is the ultimate confrontation between protagonist and antagonist at peak tension, resolving the central story question of whether Michelle will survive and what consequences Teddy faces. This scene represents the highest dramatic stakes and the final unavoidable confrontation that pays off the entire kidnapping narrative.

scenes.heading: EXT. FIELD. EARLY MORNING 1
scenes.summary: Teddy narrates over images of a honeybee pollinating an apple blossom, then tends to a beehive in full apiarist gear while explaining that a third of our food is pollinated by bees.

scenes.heading: EXT. FIELD - MORNING 1A
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don walk toward the house while removing their apiarist helmets.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEDDY’S HOUSE - PORCH. DAY 2
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don place their beekeeping gear on the porch while Teddy dismisses conspiracy theories about colony collapse disorder, mocking the idea that governments could organize such a plot, though Don doesn't follow his reasoning.

scenes.heading: INT. TEDDY’S HOUSE - LAUNDRY ROOM. DAY 3
scenes.summary: Teddy pulls clean towels from the dryer while narrating that he searched for a larger organizing principle behind the bee crisis.

scenes.heading: INT. TEDDY’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM. DAY 4
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don spread bath towels on the living room floor while Teddy narrates that he studied until he found the answer.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE'S MANSION - BEDROOM. EARLY MORNING 5
scenes.summary: Michelle Fuller wakes up in her modern, expensive bedroom just before her alarm goes off while Teddy narrates about something caging and poisoning humanity.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE’S MANSION - BATHROOM. EARLY MORNING 5A
scenes.summary: Michelle finishes brushing her teeth and looks at herself in the mirror.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE'S MANSION - BEDROOM. EARLY MORNING 5B
scenes.summary: Michelle checks emails on her phone while wearing a red-light therapy mask as Teddy narrates about civil innovations feeding back to nothing and politics having no correlation with public interest.

scenes.heading: EXT. MICHELLE'S MANSION - GARDEN. MORNING 5C
scenes.summary: Michelle does mindful stretching and meditation on a yoga mat while Teddy narrates that 'they' are steering the ship and he and Don must stop them.

scenes.heading: INT. TEDDY'S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM. DAY 6
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don stretch on bath towels while Teddy warns that 'it' will try to dominate them, and Don asks fearfully if 'she' would try to hurt them.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE'S MANSION - GARDEN. MORNING 7
scenes.summary: Michelle does Krav Maga training with intensity, appearing to fight herself, while Teddy narrates that 'it' is highly dangerous and they must prepare their brains.

scenes.heading: INT. TEDDY’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM. DAY 8
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don do sit-ups and pushups together, with Teddy encouraging Don to keep up.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE’S MANSION - GYM. MORNING 8A
scenes.summary: Michelle runs on a treadmill wearing a VO2 max measurement mask while Teddy narrates that 'it' won't play fair so they have to be better.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE'S MANSION - POOL. MORNING 8B
scenes.summary: Michelle dives into her pool and begins swimming.

scenes.heading: EXT. WOODS. MORNING 9
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don run in the woods with Don lagging behind breathless, while Teddy points up and warns that 'they' track 'it' remotely and will try to track them too.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE'S MANSION - KITCHEN. EARLY MORNING 10
scenes.summary: Michelle opens a drawer full of labeled supplement canisters, takes out today's pills, and blends a powdered smoothie while washing down handfuls of vitamins and supplements as Teddy narrates about fortifying against mental intrusion.

scenes.heading: INT. TEDDY'S HOUSE - KITCHEN. DAY 11
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don eat a mixture of honey, yogurt, and Cap'n Crunch in Teddy's kitchen while Teddy reassures Don not to apologize and tells him none of this is his fault.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE'S MANSION - DRIVEWAY. MORNING 12
scenes.summary: Michelle gets into an electric luxury car in her driveway while Teddy narrates that 'they' want people to think they made this world.

scenes.heading: EXT. STRIP MALL - PARKING LOT. DAY 13
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don stand near their bicycles in a strip mall parking lot while Don asks how to tell 'them' apart and Teddy explains the people around them are 'hollowed' and harmless.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE'S CAR. MORNING 14
scenes.summary: Michelle drives to work while Teddy narrates that 'they' planned to make humans the same as the bees.

scenes.heading: EXT. AFFLUENT SUBURBS. DAY 14A
scenes.summary: Michelle drives through an affluent suburban neighborhood.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUBURBAN STREET. MORNING 15
scenes.summary: Michelle's car travels through suburban sprawl while Teddy narrates about a dead colony broken and atomized.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY. MORNING. 15A
scenes.summary: Michelle's car drives with the city skyline in the background.

scenes.heading: EXT. AUXOLITH BIOMEDICAL HEADQUARTERS. DAY 16
scenes.summary: Michelle drives up to Auxolith Biomedical headquarters and hands her keys to a corporate valet while Teddy narrates about having no way home.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. AUXOLITH BIOMEDICAL HEADQUARTERS. DAY 16A
scenes.summary: Michelle enters the lobby, takes the elevator, and walks across the mezzanine.

scenes.heading: INT. PHARMACY. DAY 17
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don browse the pharmacy allergy aisle, with Teddy scooping dozens of antihistamine sprays into his basket and telling Don to grab cream for mitigation.

scenes.heading: EXT. STRIP MALL. DAY 17A
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don walk between the pharmacy and toy store carrying their purchased goods.

scenes.heading: INT. AUXOLITH - GLASS CONFERENCE ROOM. MORNING 18
scenes.summary: A makeup artist applies foundation to Michelle's face while a video crew sets up for taping as Teddy narrates that this is 'their' plan and he's proved it.

scenes.heading: INT. TOY STORE. DAY 19
scenes.summary: Teddy grabs two plastic Halloween masks in a toy store while Don asks in voiceover what happens if someone finds out what they're doing.

scenes.heading: EXT. STRIP MALL. DAY 19A
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don walk between the toy store and hardware store carrying their purchased goods.

scenes.heading: INT. HARDWARE STORE. DAY 20
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don load cable wire, clasps, screws, and hinges into a basket while Teddy narrates that no one on Earth cares about them.

scenes.heading: EXT. STRIP MALL - PARKING LOT. DAY 20A
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don pack their purchases into backpacks, mount their bikes, and ride off from the strip mall parking lot.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN. DAY 21
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don eat frozen taquitos while Teddy instructs Don to clear the psychic cache and avoid distractions, then steadies Don's shaking hands and tells him he's smart and brave and that no one else sees it but him.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEDDY’S BACKYARD. NIGHT 22
scenes.summary: Teddy shows Don a syringe of chemical castration medication in the backyard at night, explaining they must clear their heads of psychic compulsions to achieve maximum focus, while Don expresses uncertainty and sadness about wanting to be with someone one day.
scenes.turningPointNote: Teddy reveals the extreme measures he's willing to take for their mission, including chemical castration, escalating the disturbing nature of his plan and Don's complicity.

scenes.heading: INT. AUXOLITH - GLASS CONFERENCE ROOM. DAY 23
scenes.summary: Michelle speaks into camera in a glossy product shot, delivering a polished speech about diversity in hiring and diverse thinking as the embodiment of a modern progressive CEO.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE’S OFFICE. DAY 24
scenes.summary: Michelle prepares to leave her tasteful office while her assistant Corey runs through her schedule, including an investor call and a VC meeting with Wired.

scenes.heading: EXT. AUXOLITH HEADQUARTERS - FRONT ENTRANCE. DAY 25
scenes.summary: Michelle exits the building and receives her car keys from the corporate valet Tony, telling him he can leave early in a way that doesn't feel like his choice.

scenes.heading: EXT. AUXOLITH HEADQUARTERS - FRONT GATE. DAY 26
scenes.summary: Michelle's car pulls out of the Auxolith headquarters front gate.

scenes.heading: EXT. AFFLUENT SUBURBS. DAY 26A
scenes.summary: Michelle drives through affluent suburbs.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE’S CAR. DAY 27
scenes.summary: Michelle drives through upscale suburbs, pops a pill bottle and downs several tablets with a hint of dependency, while singing along to Steely Dan on the stereo.

scenes.heading: EXT. MICHELLE’S MANSION - STREET LEVEL. DAY 28
scenes.summary: Michelle's car pulls up to her gated mansion, and as the gate opens and she drives in, two crouched figures in concealed clothing emerge from the greenery and follow behind the car before the gate closes.
scenes.turningPointNote: The kidnapping plan moves from preparation to execution as Teddy and Don infiltrate Michelle's property, marking the shift from planning to action.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE’S CAR. DAY 29
scenes.summary: Michelle is shown head-on pulling into her driveway while the two crouched figures are visible out of focus in the window behind her, peeling off to the side.

scenes.heading: EXT. MICHELLE’S MANSION - FRONT DRIVE. DAY 30
scenes.summary: Michelle gets out of her car and is jumped by Teddy and Don in ski jackets and Halloween masks, but she throws Teddy off with surprising strength, gives Don a Krav Maga elbow strike to the throat that drops him choking, runs for the door but realizes she's lost her keys, then runs around the side of the house before Teddy tackles her and sprays Benadryl in her face until she passes out.
scenes.turningPointNote: The kidnapping succeeds despite Michelle's fierce resistance, irrevocably crossing the line from conspiracy theory to violent crime and setting the central conflict in motion.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE’S CAR. DAY 31
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don load unconscious Michelle into the backseat of her car, with Teddy getting in the driver's seat and directing Don to stay in back and spray Benadryl, then they remove their masks, put on EMF-protection beanies, and drive off as 'Babylon Sisters' plays.

scenes.heading: EXT. MICHELLE’S MANSION - STREET LEVEL. DAY 32
scenes.summary: Michelle's car pulls out of the open gate.

scenes.heading: EXT. RESIDENTIAL STREET. DAY 33
scenes.summary: Michelle's car drives cautiously through quiet affluent suburbs with tinted windows preventing anyone from seeing inside.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE’S CAR. DAY. 34
scenes.summary: Teddy nervously checks the rear-view mirror while Don struggles with electric clippers to shave Michelle's head, with Teddy yelling that her hair is like GPS and they can be tracked remotely, until Don finally runs the clippers more forcefully and shaves her completely bald.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY. DAY 35
scenes.summary: The car drives on the highway heading into rural hinterlands.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE’S CAR. DAY. 36
scenes.summary: Teddy looks up at grey angry clouds unable to see the sky, Don finishes shaving Michelle's head completely bald, Michelle's phone rings showing a call from Corey, Teddy identifies the caller as 'them' and smashes the phone with a hammer.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY. DAY 36A
scenes.summary: Teddy jerks the steering wheel to correct the car's course on the highway.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE’S CAR. DAY 36B
scenes.summary: Teddy violently smashes Michelle's phone with clippers until it shatters, then calms down while Don watches fearfully.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY. DAY 36C
scenes.summary: A police car flashes its lights in response to Teddy's erratic driving on the highway.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE’S CAR. DAY 36D
scenes.summary: Teddy sees the cop car in the mirror and curses as it speeds up behind them to pull them over.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY. DAY 37
scenes.summary: Teddy fakes pulling over then guns it onto an exit, causing the police car to slam into the divider.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE'S CAR. DAY 38
scenes.summary: Teddy floors it through side streets, exhilarated and shouting at the police while Don asks if they're okay.

scenes.heading: EXT. SMALL TOWN. DAY 38A
scenes.summary: The car speeds through a small town street.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE’S CAR. DAY. 38B
scenes.summary: Teddy drives fast while Don looks a little worried.

scenes.heading: EXT. INTERSECTION. DAY 39
scenes.summary: Michelle's car runs a red light at an empty intersection and turns onto the road leading to Teddy's house.

scenes.heading: EXT. WOODED AREA. TWILIGHT 40
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don conceal Michelle's car with branches, burn Michelle's hair and phone in a fire, and sit together as Teddy praises Don and tells him they'll save the world.
scenes.turningPointNote: The kidnapping is complete and the cousins have successfully hidden Michelle and destroyed evidence, marking the transition from abduction to captivity.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEDDY’S HOUSE. NIGHT 41
scenes.summary: Darkness falls outside Teddy's house.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT. NIGHT 42
scenes.summary: Michelle lies unconscious in the basement, restrained with metal bracelets and wires bolted to the wall, while Teddy and Don survey her and Teddy insists she's not real.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT. NIGHT 42C
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don smear thick white anti-itch cream all over Michelle's unconscious face and body while Teddy explains it weakens her nervous system.

scenes.heading: INT. TEDDY’S BEDROOM. EVENING 43
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don put on ill-fitting suits, with Teddy insisting Don's tight suit was left by his father for a reason.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT. NIGHT 44
scenes.summary: Michelle regains consciousness in the basement as Teddy reads her a prepared statement about the human resistance and Geneva Convention, then demands she request an audience with her emperor to discuss her species' withdrawal from Earth.
scenes.turningPointNote: First interrogation scene establishes the delusional framework of Teddy's belief system and Michelle's captive reality.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN. NIGHT 45
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don emerge from the basement, lock the door, then laugh and hug each other in celebration of pulling off the kidnapping.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEDDY'S HOUSE. NIGHT 45A
scenes.summary: The house sits quietly with lit windows at night.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN. NIGHT 46
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don celebrate with taquitos while watching Michelle cry on a monitor, with Teddy insisting they must see her as the Fuller Humanoid and reminding Don she's responsible for honeybee deaths.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM. NIGHT 47
scenes.summary: Don lies alone in the dark listening to Michelle weeping through the air vent and begins crying himself, covering his ears.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN. NIGHT 48
scenes.summary: Teddy sits in the dark kitchen watching Michelle on the monitor, then stares at a spot on the floor.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN. MANY YEARS AGO. DAY B+W 49
scenes.summary: In a flashback, young Teddy gently removes needles from his strung-out mother Sandy's body while she tells him she doesn't even know who his father is.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN. PRESENT DAY. NIGHT 50
scenes.summary: Teddy nearly cries but scrunches his face and shuts out the emotion.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE 51
scenes.summary: A title card indicates it is three days before the lunar eclipse.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT. EARLY MORNING 51A
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don reapply anti-itch cream all over Michelle's face and body.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEDDY’S HOUSE. EARLY MORNING 52
scenes.summary: Teddy gets on his bike, puts in earbuds, and heads out.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET. EARLY MORNING 53
scenes.summary: Teddy pedals furiously through the bleak post-industrial landscape while listening to a podcast about aliens treating humans as a resource.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY. EARLY MORNING 54
scenes.summary: A police cruiser flashes its lights behind Teddy, causing him to panic, but the officer only stops to check if he's okay and offers him a ride, which Teddy declines.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKING LOT. EARLY MORNING 55
scenes.summary: Teddy pulls into the parking lot of Auxolith Biomedical fulfillment center.

scenes.heading: INT. AUXOLITH FULFILLMENT CENTER. DAY 56
scenes.summary: Teddy walks through the grim warehouse, greeting tired coworkers warmly and hugging an exhausted older woman named Tina.

scenes.heading: INT. BREAK ROOM. DAY 57
scenes.summary: Teddy sets out homemade honey for coworkers, then notices a muted TV news program showing Michelle's photo and her car before being slapped on the back by Carlos.

scenes.heading: INT. NEWS STUDIO 57A *
scenes.summary: A news reporter describes Michelle Fuller's disappearance after leaving work Monday afternoon and announces a statewide manhunt is underway.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET. DAY 58
scenes.summary: Teddy bikes home listening to a self-help podcast about funneling trauma into creativity.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEDDY’S HOUSE - BEEHIVES. DAY 59
scenes.summary: Teddy tends lovingly to his honeybee colony.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEDDY'S HOUSE. DAY. 59A
scenes.summary: Establishing shots of Teddy's house.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN. DAY 60
scenes.summary: Don reports to Teddy that Michelle hasn't eaten or used the bedpan, and Teddy insists it's a ploy to trap them with pity.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT. DAY 61
scenes.summary: Teddy plays back Michelle's sarcastic recording claiming to be an alien, rejects it as inadequate, and when she refuses to comply seriously, he violently attacks her with the shotgun butt, beating her repeatedly while Don watches in horror.
scenes.turningPointNote: Teddy's violence escalates from psychological to physical brutality, crossing a threshold that horrifies even Don and reveals the true danger of Teddy's delusion.

scenes.heading: EXT. DRIVEWAY. DAY 62 *
scenes.summary: Teddy storms outside and violently stomps on the roof of his mother's station wagon before collapsing in exhaustion, then accepts comfort from Don.

scenes.heading: EXT. COSMOS RENDERING 63
scenes.summary: A cheap CG rendering shows the Andromeda Galaxy as Teddy's voiceover claims credit for discovering how to identify Andromedans.

scenes.heading: INT. SPACESHIP RENDERING 64
scenes.summary: A primitive CG rendering shows the interior of Teddy's imagined Andromedan mothership where negotiations will take place.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEDDY'S HOUSE. NIGHT 65
scenes.summary: Teddy shows Don the CG render and explains they'll only be in space for a few hours to save Earth, disappointing Don who thought they'd leave permanently.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE 66
scenes.summary: A title card indicates it is two days before the lunar eclipse.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT. DAY 67
scenes.summary: Michelle nearly breaks free from her chains but stops when Teddy and Don enter, then eagerly claims to be an alien and offers to help, but Teddy remains silent and unmoved by her attempts.

scenes.heading: EXT. AUXOLITH HQ. DAY B+W 68
scenes.summary: In a flashback, a younger Teddy with purple hair sits with his comatose mother in a wheelchair as Michelle apologizes for what happened and offers a settlement, while 'Good Morning Starshine' plays.
scenes.turningPointNote: Reveals the prior connection between Teddy and Michelle, showing she was involved in an incident that left his mother comatose, providing crucial context for his obsession.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT. PRESENT DAY. DAY 69
scenes.summary: Michelle stares at the boombox, remembers Teddy saying she can't talk her way out again, and her mind begins working on a plan.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE 70
scenes.summary: A title card indicates it is one day before the lunar eclipse.

scenes.heading: INT. SUPERMARKET. DAY 71
scenes.summary: Teddy shops for expensive spaghetti, tomato sauce, frozen coconut cake, and cheap red wine at a supermarket.

scenes.heading: INT. THRIFT STORE. DAY 71A
scenes.summary: Teddy stares at various wigs in a thrift store.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET. DAY 71B
scenes.summary: Teddy cycles home with purchases in his backpack.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN. DAY 72
scenes.summary: Teddy and Don set a fancy table with their best plates and candle, with Teddy explaining they'll give Michelle the decorum she expects to earn her trust.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT. DAY 73
scenes.summary: Teddy unlocks Michelle and motions for her to follow him upstairs while Don keeps watch with the shotgun.

scenes.heading: EXT. BATHROOM. DAY 74
scenes.summary: Don stands guard outside the bathroom door with the shotgun.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN. DAY 74A
scenes.summary: Teddy nails a dining chair to the floor in the kitchen.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM. DAY 75
scenes.summary: Michelle showers and discovers her clothes have been cleaned and folded, with a replacement shirt marked 'S.G.' that belonged to Teddy's mother.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN. DAY 76
scenes.summary: Michelle sits handcuffed to the nailed chair at a candlelit dinner while Don guards her with a shotgun; Teddy serves spaghetti and apologizes for not recognizing her royal status, treating her as if she shares blood with an emperor.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT DOOR. CONTINUOUS. DAY 77
scenes.summary: Casey arrives at Teddy's front door asking about a missing persons case, causing Teddy slight concern.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT. DAY 78
scenes.summary: Michelle wakes in the basement guarded by Don with the shotgun and tries to talk to him, but he refuses to look at her or respond.

scenes.heading: INT. TEDDY’S LIVING ROOM. DAY 79
scenes.summary: Casey looks around Teddy's living room at the UFO paraphernalia while Teddy offers him water.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN. CONTINUOUS. DAY 80
scenes.summary: Teddy enters the kitchen and quickly switches off the monitor showing Michelle and Don downstairs; Casey notices nails in the floor and Teddy claims his mother had fits, then Casey spots a frozen cake and begins asking about Auxolith.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT. CONTINUOUS. DAY 81
scenes.summary: Michelle tells Don he behaved heroically by not shooting her and that he's been stopping Teddy from harming her, causing Don to finally crack and speak to her.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN. DAY 82
scenes.summary: Teddy and Casey share coconut cake and wine by the window in the rain; Casey apologizes for bullying Teddy years ago, explaining it was a power thing.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT. DAY 83
scenes.summary: Michelle tells Don the police are upstairs and it's not too late for him to let her go, but Don insists Teddy is all he has and that he loves him; when Michelle asks where he wants to go, Don asks if she would help him as an alien.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEDDY’S HOUSE. DAY 84
scenes.summary: Teddy and Casey walk toward the beehives in apiarist suits; Teddy gives Casey a self-published UFO book and they put on helmets to inspect the bees.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT. DAY 85
scenes.summary: Don puts the shotgun barrel under his chin and shoots himself, spraying blood and brain matter onto Michelle.
scenes.turningPointNote: Don's suicide eliminates Michelle's guard and potential ally, fundamentally altering the power dynamic and triggering the violent climax of Act 2.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEDDY’S HOUSE - BEEHIVES. DAY 86
scenes.summary: Teddy and Casey hear the shotgun blast from the house and Casey reacts in alarm.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT. DAY 87
scenes.summary: Michelle sits in shock with blood splattered on her face, staring at Don's dead body on the floor.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEDDY’S HOUSE - BEEHIVES. DAY 88
scenes.summary: Teddy removes Casey's helmet, smashes a hive tray full of bees onto his face, kicks over a beehive to swarm him, then beats Casey to death with a shovel before crawling away through the bees.
scenes.turningPointNote: Teddy murders Casey to prevent discovery, crossing into irreversible violence and eliminating any chance of outside intervention or rescue.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT. DAY 89
scenes.summary: Teddy rushes downstairs and discovers Don's body with the shotgun beside him; he cradles Don's corpse and weeps while Michelle insists Don killed himself, but Teddy looks at her with terrifying malice.

scenes.heading: EXT. WOODED AREA. DAY 90
scenes.summary: Teddy finds Michelle's car hidden in the woods, retrieves an antifreeze jug from the trunk, and puts it in his backpack.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT. DAY 91
scenes.summary: Michelle, chained to the wall with an injured leg, lies on the floor and stretches to reach Don's body but his foot remains just out of reach.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET. DAY 92
scenes.summary: Teddy pedals his bike furiously down the street.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT. DAY 93
scenes.summary: Michelle grasps Don's shoelace with her fingertips, unties it, pulls his body close enough to reach his pockets, retrieves the keys, and unlocks her restraints.

scenes.heading: EXT. LONG TERM CARE FACILITY. DAY 94
scenes.summary: Teddy arrives at Evergreen Extended Care Community on his bike and rushes inside.

scenes.heading: INT. LONG TERM CARE FACILITY. DAY 95
scenes.summary: Teddy approaches Sandy's room and slips inside.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT. DAY 96
scenes.summary: Michelle repeatedly heaves herself against the locked basement door but cannot break through; she collapses, makes a splint for her leg, and from a new angle notices a bookcase filled with books on political science, ecology, and the paranormal.

scenes.heading: INT. LONG TERM CARE FACILITY - SANDY’S ROOM. DAY 97
scenes.summary: Teddy shuts the door to his comatose mother's room, rushes to her side, and touches her cheek.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT. DAY 98
scenes.summary: Michelle shoves the bookcase aside and opens a hidden door.

scenes.heading: INT. TEDDY’S RESEARCH ROOM. DAY 99
scenes.summary: Michelle enters Teddy's hidden research room filled with medical books, files, instruments, and shelves of preserved human organs in jars.
scenes.turningPointNote: Michelle discovers Teddy's secret laboratory with preserved human organs, revealing the full scope of his delusional experiments and past murders.

scenes.heading: INT. LONG TERM CARE FACILITY - SANDY’S ROOM. DAY 100
scenes.summary: Teddy fiddles with his mother's IV drip bag and unscrews the antifreeze jug cap.

scenes.heading: INT. TEDDY’S RESEARCH ROOM. DAY 101
scenes.summary: Michelle finds Teddy's workstation with dried blood, pulls binder number one from a shelf, and sees photos and notes documenting a man chained to the wall with a shaved head and cream on his face, along with autopsy photos and anatomical drawings.

scenes.heading: INT. LONG TERM CARE FACILITY - SANDY’S ROOM. DAY 102
scenes.summary: Antifreeze feeds into Sandy's IV tube as Teddy anxiously watches his mother for any sign of revival.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM. YEARS AGO. DAY B+W 103
scenes.summary: In a flashback, Sandy frantically wraps young Teddy in silver space blankets, telling him they will protect him from people who aren't human.

scenes.heading: INT. LONG TERM CARE FACILITY - SANDY’S ROOM. DAY 104
scenes.summary: Teddy sees his mother's finger twitch, then multiple fingers, then her entire hand move, and his face lights up with hope.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM. YEARS AGO. DAY B+W 105
scenes.summary: In flashback, Sandy inflates the silver blankets around Teddy like a balloon while warning him about antenna heads breaking people down, then demands he promise to look for signs in the sky.

scenes.heading: INT. LONG TERM CARE FACILITY - SANDY’S ROOM. DAY 106
scenes.summary: Sandy's twitches turn into violent convulsions across her entire body as Teddy tries desperately to control them.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM. YEARS AGO. DAY B+W 107
scenes.summary: In flashback, Sandy pumps the foot pump in reverse, vacuum-sealing the silver material tight over young Teddy's face and suffocating him as he promises to look for signs.

scenes.heading: INT. LONG TERM CARE FACILITY - SANDY’S ROOM. DAY 108
scenes.summary: Sandy's heart monitor flatlines; Teddy cries and kisses her hands as she dies, then panics at noises from the hallway and climbs out the window.
scenes.turningPointNote: Teddy's mother dies from the antifreeze he administered, completing his descent into murderous delusion and eliminating his last human connection.

scenes.heading: EXT. LONG TERM CARE FACILITY. DAY 109
scenes.summary: Teddy runs from the facility in tears, then turns to watch nurses rush to his mother's room; his sadness slowly transforms into blind rage.

scenes.heading: INT. TEDDY’S RESEARCH ROOM. DAY 110
scenes.summary: Michelle surveys Teddy's research room filled with evidence of his violent search for answers and realizes what she must do to escape.
scenes.turningPointNote: Michelle decides to pretend to be an alien to manipulate Teddy, shifting from victim to active strategist in a desperate gambit for survival.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET. DAY 111
scenes.summary: Teddy bikes home with furious tears in his eyes.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEDDY’S HOUSE. DAY 112
scenes.summary: Teddy leaps off his bike and runs into the house.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN. DAY 113
scenes.summary: Teddy grabs the shotgun from the kitchen table and runs downstairs making wild, savage noises.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT. DAY 114
scenes.summary: Teddy enters to find Michelle standing unchained with otherworldly authority; she demands to know how many of his victims were Andromedans, he admits two, and she confidently steps forward calling him a miserable idiot while he realizes his research has been discovered.
scenes.turningPointNote: Michelle fully commits to the alien deception, dominating Teddy psychologically and beginning the pitch that will determine whether she lives or dies.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE 115
scenes.summary: The Earth is shown from space with a title card indicating the night of the lunar eclipse.

scenes.heading: EXT. AUXOLITH BIOMEDICAL HEADQUARTERS - FRONT GATE. DAY 116
scenes.summary: Michelle drives up to the Auxolith gate wearing a wig and her dirtied suit; the stunned security guard recognizes her and she asks to be buzzed through, claiming everything is sorted out.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE’S CAR. DAY. 117
scenes.summary: Teddy hides in the back seat with the shotgun trained on Michelle and instructs her to park in her usual spot; Don's dead body wrapped in a blanket sits in the trunk as Teddy promises to take him along.

scenes.heading: INT. AUXOLITH HEADQUARTERS - FRONT ENTRANCE. DAY 118
scenes.summary: Teddy exits the car wearing a long overcoat, hands Michelle crutches, and she crutches toward the stunned valet to hand him her keys.

scenes.heading: INT. AUXOLITH HEADQUARTERS. DAY 119
scenes.summary: Teddy and Michelle enter the Auxolith building.

scenes.heading: INT. AUXOLITH HEADQUARTERS - ELEVATOR. DAY 120
scenes.summary: Teddy grips the shotgun hidden in his overcoat during the elevator ride.

scenes.heading: INT. AUXOLITH HEADQUARTERS - EXECUTIVE FLOOR. DAY 121
scenes.summary: Michelle crutches past stunned colleagues on the executive floor, nervously assuring them everything is good and suggesting some can go home; Teddy trails behind concealing the shotgun as Michelle tells Corey she needs privacy.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE’S OFFICE. DAY 122
scenes.summary: Michelle and Teddy enter her office; he locks the door, barricades it with a couch, and closes the blinds; Michelle claims they will make remote contact with the emperor using a calculator with a 58-digit code, and begins typing while Teddy watches incredulously.
scenes.turningPointNote: Michelle executes her final desperate con, attempting to convince Teddy she can contact aliens via calculator while trapped in her office with him and a shotgun.

scenes.heading: INT. AUXOLITH HEADQUARTERS - EXECUTIVE FLOOR. DAY 123
scenes.summary: Police and SWAT officers gather outside Michelle's evacuated office, preparing to breach the door.

scenes.heading: INT. MICHELLE’S OFFICE. DAY 123A
scenes.summary: A loud explosion bursts open the armored closet door, and Teddy is glimpsed being cut in half; the metal door handle flies off and strikes Michelle in the head, knocking her unconscious and causing her wig to fall off, revealing her bald head.
scenes.turningPointNote: Teddy is killed in the explosion and Michelle's true identity (bald head beneath wig) is physically revealed as she loses consciousness.

scenes.heading: EXT. AUXOLITH HEADQUARTERS - PARKING LOT. TWILIGHT 124
scenes.summary: Michelle lies woozy on a stretcher with a broken foot in a brace as a cleanup crew works the crime scene; she feebly calls out her pet slogan to evacuated Auxolith employees before being loaded into an ambulance.

scenes.heading: INT. AMBULANCE. NIGHT 125
scenes.summary: Inside the moving ambulance, the EMT and detective reassure Michelle she'll be fine and can answer questions tomorrow, while she stares off with a haunted look.

scenes.heading: INT. AMBULANCE. NIGHT 126
scenes.summary: The ambulance stops at a red light.

scenes.heading: INT. AMBULANCE. NIGHT 127
scenes.summary: Michelle asks if Teddy is dead and the detective confirms he passed away from the homemade explosive, calling him too crazy to be alive, while Michelle looks genuinely mournful.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD. NIGHT 128
scenes.summary: Michelle tears off her cast, clambers from the ambulance, runs through honking traffic across the highway, and looks up to see what appears to be lightning flashing dimly in a cloud.
scenes.turningPointNote: Michelle abandons medical care and flees the ambulance with urgent purpose, searching for something in the sky.

scenes.heading: EXT. AUXOLITH BIOMEDICAL HEADQUARTERS. NIGHT 129
scenes.summary: Michelle uses her security badge to make her way back onto Auxolith property while avoiding police and fire vehicles.

scenes.heading: INT. AUXOLITH HEADQUARTERS - BACK ENTRANCE. NIGHT 130
scenes.summary: Michelle enters through a back entrance and uses her card to access a quiet staircase leading upward.

scenes.heading: INT. AUXOLITH HEADQUARTERS - EXECUTIVE FLOOR. NIGHT 131
scenes.summary: Michelle quickly grabs the calculator she dropped (with digits still on screen) from behind her office couch and starts toward the closet, but a young detective tries to stop her from entering the crime scene area.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM. NIGHT 132
scenes.summary: Michelle writhes on the floor of a gelatinous room as two men enter and cover her shaking body with a translucent sheet while she coughs and retches violently.
scenes.turningPointNote: Michelle has transported to another location/dimension and is physically adjusting to a new atmosphere, revealing the reality behind the closet portal.

scenes.heading: INT. IMPERIAL THRONE ROOM. DAY 133
scenes.summary: Michelle, now addressed as Emperor, meets with the Andromedan Royal Court in a throne room and learns that experiments on human subjects have nearly all failed with only two remaining, and there is no likelihood of success.
scenes.turningPointNote: Michelle is revealed to be an alien Emperor leading experiments on humanity; the failure of these experiments sets the stage for what follows.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET. DAY 133A
scenes.summary: A street is shown full of dead people lying on the ground.

scenes.heading: INT. BRITISH SCHOOL CLASSROOM. DAY 133B
scenes.summary: A British school classroom is shown full of dead children.

scenes.heading: INT. KOREAN SCHOOL CLASSROOM. DAY 133C
scenes.summary: A Korean school classroom is shown full of dead children.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPORT. DAY 133D
scenes.summary: Dead bodies pile up at the end of a long moving walkway at an airport.

scenes.heading: EXT. ACROPOLIS OF ATHENS. DAY 133E
scenes.summary: Construction workers lie dead at the Acropolis in Athens.

scenes.heading: INT. CATHEDRAL. DAY 133F
scenes.summary: Everybody at a small Russian wedding in a cathedral is dead.

scenes.heading: INT. NIGHTCLUB. NIGHT 133G
scenes.summary: Everybody at a techno party is dead while a loud bass note repeats, strobe lights flicker, and a smoke machine blows smoke into nothingness.

scenes.heading: INT. SMITHFIELD MEAT MARKET. DAY 133H
scenes.summary: Dead humans lie amidst hanging meat at Smithfield meat market.

scenes.heading: INT. MUSEUM. DAY 133I
scenes.summary: An entire family lies dead with melting ice cream in the kids' hands during a day out at a museum.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. BUILDING SITE. DAY 133J
scenes.summary: Business men in ties and suits lie dead in the middle of opening a new building site.

scenes.heading: EXT. AMAZON RAINFOREST. DAY 133K
scenes.summary: An indigenous man lies dead on the Amazon.

scenes.heading: EXT. LANDFILL IN INDIA. DAY 133L
scenes.summary: Children collecting rubbish from a landfill in India lie dead on a rubbish pile.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL GRANDE BRETAGNE, ATHENS. DAY 133M
scenes.summary: A rich man lies dead in the bath of the Royal Suite in the Hotel Grande Bretagne, Athens.

scenes.heading: INT. AA MEETING ROOM. DAY 133N
scenes.summary: A circle of alcoholics sit dead in their chairs at an AA meeting.

scenes.heading: INT. TOY FACTORY, CHINA. DAY 133O
scenes.summary: Chinese factory workers in matching uniforms lie dead mid-work amongst huge piles of stuffed toys.

scenes.heading: EXT. CEMETERY. DAY 133P
scenes.summary: A young woman lies dead with a bouquet of flowers in her hand next to a gravestone.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM. DAY 133Q
scenes.summary: Two teenagers who had been having sex now lie dead in each other's arms.

scenes.heading: EXT. FREEWAY. DAY 133R
scenes.summary: Multiple car crashes sit on a now completely still freeway.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL, SURGERY ROOM. DAY 133S
scenes.summary: All doctors lie dead during a life-saving surgery with the patient's chest still open.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. CHURCH. DAY 133T
scenes.summary: A bride and her friends who were preparing to walk down the aisle now lie dead.

scenes.heading: INT. AUXOLITH FULFILLMENT CENTER. DAY 133U
scenes.summary: The fulfillment center where Teddy worked has stopped and his coworker Tina lies on the floor at peace.

scenes.heading: INT. TEDDY'S HOUSE. DAY 133V
scenes.summary: Inside Teddy's house all is still, with a framed photo on the wall showing young Teddy being bathed by his mother.

scenes.heading: EXT. MEADOW. DAY 134
scenes.summary: All human life is gone and the countryside is alive with sounds of insects, birds, and mammals as a honeybee lands on a flower swaying in the clean breeze.
scenes.turningPointNote: The complete extinction of humanity is revealed as the final outcome, with Earth saved through the elimination of the human species.
